user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] Daily automation: Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected. Account: Contoso Ltd.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-18 10:23 EST
Subject: Follow-up: QBR preparation Review

There's been a change in our timeline for QBR preparation. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
Date: 2024-02-04 17:40 EST
Subject: QBR preparation - Action Required

Our team encountered some challenges with QBR preparation. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-15 11:46 EST
Subject: QBR preparation - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about QBR preparation. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: QBR preparation Discussion - Contoso Ltd
Date: 2024-02-18
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd), Marcus Williams (Microsoft)

[00:01:30] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into QBR preparation.

[00:08:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on vendor dependencies and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:12:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

[00:16:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:21:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is cost optimization.

[00:26:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): The timeline for vendor dependencies is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:31:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:33:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.
